name: Federal Reserve
summary: Day 63 Thursday. FOMC HELD 3.5-3.75% WITH FOUR-WAY DISSENT — MOST SPLINTERED FOMC SINCE OCTOBER 1992. POWELL: 'IT HASN'T EVEN PEAKED YET' ON ENERGY INFLATION = EXPLICIT STRUCTURAL FRAMING (not transitory). 11-1 vote on rate (Stephen Miran lone Trump-nominee dovish dissent for 25bp cut); 4 dissents on statement collectively (Hammack/Cleveland, Kashkari/Minneapolis, Logan/Dallas voted with majority on rate but OPPOSED inclusion of easing bias — HAWKISH dissents). The 3:1 hawkish-vs-dovish dissent ratio is the operative signal — even Fed officials disagreeing with the statement language want it MORE hawkish. Powell confirmed final press conference as Chair; Warsh-transition 'very normal, standard.' POWELL'S STRUCTURAL FRAMING IS THE INVERSE OF YESTERDAY'S 'TRANSITORY' BINARY — yesterday's brief flagged 'transitory vs structural' as the single highest-information-density variable; Powell chose STRUCTURAL on the record. Cascade: DXY firmed +0.4% to 98.99 (breaks 98.7 ceiling); US 10Y +7bps to 4.42% (highest in 3M); CME FedWatch 0% Apr cut, 30% by Dec (down from 70% in Mar); 'Fed pivot is the next catalyst' narrative STRUCTURALLY REJECTED. For gold INFLATION-regime call: Day 10 of empirical confirmation; Powell's verbal validation (gold trapped under $4,600). For INR: no DXY-relief vector → ₹94.82 holds, ₹0.18 from ₹95. For Indian equities: removes DXY-relief vector flagged as potential catalyst. For US 10Y: pricing structural inflation acceptance; 4.42% close highest in 3M. Goldman gold regime call empirically vindicated Day 10. [TRUNCATED — needs re-synthesis]
